message:
feat(core): add single-indirect block pointer write for e2compr

Added write_block_ptr() that handles both direct blocks (0-11) and
single-indirect blocks (12 through 12+ptrs_per_block-1). For the
indirect level:
- Allocates the indirect block (i_block[12]) on first use
- Zero-fills the new block
- Read-modify-writes the pointer at the correct offset
- Stages writes through the transaction if one is active

All e2compr write callers (write_ext4_compressed_cluster,
write_ext4_uncompressed_cluster) now use write_block_ptr instead of
the direct-only set_indirect_block_ptr.

For 4K blocks with 4-block clusters (shift=2), this extends the
maximum compressible file size from 48KB to ~4MB. Double/triple
indirect support is deferred (returns clear UnsupportedFeature error).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
